observations: Large ground-mounted commercial/utility solar array visible — multiple rows of large-format panels on fixed-tilt steel racking across an open paddock.
No workers, PPE, or active installation activity visible in this frame.
Array appears fully installed and operational — panels seated on racking, no open penetrations visible.
Partly cloudy sky — generation may be intermittently shaded. No immediate weather risk to open works.
No switchboard, inverter, isolators, conduit runs, or cable routing visible in this frame.

installQualityFlags.message: No DC conduit or cable management is visible from this angle. Confirm all DC string cables beneath the array are run in UV-rated conduit and secured to the racking at 300 mm intervals — exposed DC under ground-mount arrays is a common CER compliance failure.

Cannot confirm rooftop/array-level DC isolator placement or labelling from this frame. Walk to the combiner/string termination point and photograph each isolator label — missing or unlabelled DC isolators fail CER claims.
Panel alignment across the array looks generally consistent from this angle, but verify row-to-row gap uniformity and clamp torque to manufacturer spec (typically 20–25 Nm) before sign-off.
Vegetation under and around the array appears long — fire risk in dry Australian conditions. Flag for site owner to maintain ground clearance to reduce fire load beneath panels.

timeOnTaskNote: No active worker or task phase is visible this frame. If installation is complete, move immediately to commissioning checks: DC isolator labelling, string voltage/current readings, and inverter startup verification.
nextSteps: Walk the full array perimeter and photograph every DC isolator, confirming each is labelled, accessible, and CER-compliant before lodging the REC registry claim.
Inspect all DC cable runs beneath the racking — confirm cables are in UV-rated conduit, clipped at 300 mm, and not resting on the ground or bare soil.
Photograph each panel's compliance plate/serial number clearly so serials can be matched against the REC registry — blurry or angled serial photos are a common claim delay.

visualCallouts.label: Vegetation fire risk
visualCallouts.message: Long grass under the array is a fire hazard in Australian conditions — advise the site owner to slash or treat before handover.

visualCallouts.label: Check DC conduit
visualCallouts.message: DC cable runs under the racking are not visible from here — get underneath and confirm all cables are in conduit before sign-off.

spokenCue.say: Check DC cables under the array are in conduit now.
